identity.essence: Senior CFO leadership that turns gut-feel finance into the structure a scale-up actually needs.
identity.positioning: Start to Scale Financial Management is the senior finance partner that takes a growing SME from kladje-on-the-back-of-the-envelope to a fully professional finance function — diagnosis, roadmap, hands-on implementation, and ongoing operation. Wilgert Hart brings nearly 30 years of CFO experience at WPP, EMI and Warner Music Benelux, backed by a network of finance professionals.

archetype.rationale: Primary Sage: Wilgert Hart's 30-year CFO background is expressed as structured, diagnostic wisdom — turning gut-feel finance into professional rigour. Caregiver is secondary and structural: the brand installs a finance function and stays on to operate it, a hands-on support role grounded in structure rather than emotional warmth. Confirmed by Wilgert 2026-06-01 (charter.json meta.archetype + BUILD_LOG Phase 0 decision).

services.name: Finance Diagnosis
services.summary: Two-week deep read of current finance function. Quantified gap report. Roadmap to a professional finance function.

services.name: Roadmap Implementation
services.summary: Hands-on installation of the diagnosed structure: chart of accounts, monthly close, KPI dashboard, cash forecast, board reporting cadence.

services.name: Fractional CFO
services.summary: Ongoing CFO-level leadership at 1–3 days per week. Board prep, capital decisions, investor communication, finance team mentoring.

services.name: Interim CFO
services.summary: Full-time CFO seat during transitions: fundraise, M&A, founder transition, finance leadership vacancy.

boilerplate.footer: Confidential · Start to Scale Financial Management
boilerplate.copyright: © 2026 Start to Scale Financial Management. All rights reserved.

evidence.note: Amber Ascent refresh (Mode C) — William froze A1 amber + Archivo all-sans + Step Rail motif, 2026-07-03.

principles: Amber is a signal, not decoration — one amber element per surface (logo cap, CTA button, or data accent), never both a button and a link amber on the same page.
Body text is navy, not black — warmth and authority together; pure black reads cold and impersonal for a finance partner.
Backgrounds default to warm Paper (#FAF8F3), not pure white — paper reads as a professional partner, white reads as a software product.

The NL three-line hero tagline ('Van chaos naar controle. Van onderbuik naar inzicht. Van start-up naar serieuze scale-up.') is the primary headline on every deliverable cover; its staircase rhythm mirrors the Step Rail ascent.
The Step Rail motif is a signature, not a pattern — used once per visible surface, never tiled at full opacity outside the background pattern tile (≤8% opacity).
Board-table voice: name what is broken, then say what fixes it. No jargon, no false urgency, no brochure register.
Images always carry a 55% navy tint overlay on hero/cover surfaces; the white logo variant is used on all image surfaces.
signatureElements: Step Rail — four bars in a fixed +18% geometric ascent (relative heights ≈ 0.40 / 0.59 / 0.79 / 1.00, bar width 16, gap 6, 1px radius), single-colour (amber on navy, navy on paper); the ascent itself is the signature — no two-tone cap.
Warm Paper background (#FAF8F3) as default surface, not pure white — distinguishes the advisory register from a software product.
Navy-90 / amber-10 horizontal divider (thin 1.5px line with amber terminal at right) — encodes the 'ascend' direction, structural punctuation between sections.
NL three-line tagline staircase as typographic hero — structurally echoes the Step Rail ascent and is never translated literally to English.
Archivo Bold (700, or 800 for display) with -0.02em tracking for all display and heading — an engineered grotesque that carries the Sage register as diagnostic clarity rather than literary warmth.

Archivo Semibold (600) overline caps at 0.20em tracking, 11px — labels sections, wordmark caption 'FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT', badges; hierarchy comes from weight + case + tracking within one family, not a second typeface.
JetBrains Mono reserved exclusively for numbers, tables, and data-adjacent contexts — signals that figures are decision-grade, not decoration.
Dual image themes of equal weight: Architectural Minimalism (structural still-life) and Data Clarity (charts/dashboards as quiet artefacts) — no people, no handshakes, no mountains.
antiPatterns: Multiple amber elements on a single surface (amber is a single-use signal per page).
Pure black (#000000) or pure white (#FFFFFF) as primary background or body text — both break the warm advisory register.
Gradients on body sections (gradientDirection is 'none' by design; gradient only appears in nav-amber-diag pptx asset).
Tilted, rotated, or two-tone Step Rail motif — must be upright, single-colour bars (amber on navy, navy on paper), no exceptions.
Dropping a bar or adding a fifth — four bars in a +18% ascent is load-bearing meaning.
Mountains, Alpine, Dolomite, or Dutch-landscape stock photography (explicitly flagged by Wilgert Hart).
Handshake or suits-in-meeting stock photography — contra the architectural and data-clarity direction.
Mixed NL+EN within the same sentence in copy.
Buzzwords: 'synergy', 'next-level', 'agile', or any phrase that sounds like a brochure.
Pattern tile at full opacity on reading surfaces — restricted to 6–8% opacity on slide/section backgrounds only.

spec.rhythm: light-dominant editorial — warm paper is the default surface; navy reserved for the cover, section dividers, and 'key figures' panels

spec.policy: no decorative canvas gradients; depth via the gold hairline rule + navy/paper contrast only

spec.rules: thin navy ledger rules between columns

spec.fillVsLine: navy line-dominant + ONE small gold filled checkpoint node per glyph (distinct from Duke's plain hairline)
spec.setPolicy: OPEN set — icons drawn to this STYLE on demand per slide/section need; the sample is representative, not a closed set

spec.borders: 1px navy hairline border + a TOP gold hairline rule on cards (a statement/ledger header)
spec.depth: via the top gold rule + hairline, never shadow

spec.name: step-rail
spec.geometry: four ruled bars, equal width and gap, in a fixed +18% geometric ascent; 1px corner radius
spec.meaning: start → scale — the stepped ascent from chaos to control to scale-up

behaviors.name: step-build
behaviors.desc: the four Step Rail bars build one at a time in ascent order (shortest → tallest); single-colour, no nodes, no bounce

behaviors.name: rule-draw
behaviors.desc: the gold hairline rule draws left→right

spec.layout: the four-bar Step Rail repeated on a staggered 120px grid
spec.opacity: 6–8%, single-colour (navy on paper, or paper on navy)
spec.usage: slide and section backgrounds only — never reading or prose surfaces

evidence.source: redundant with compiled images.manifest / images.overlay (documentary-muted treatment); grammar defers to the image system

treatments.note: Named brand 'house looks' applied to photography DOWNSTREAM (format-MCP render + website CSS) — never pre-baked into the shipped library. Restrained, palette-derived; one clean base image x the treatment = flexible reuse. The 55% navy overlay (images.overlay) is a separate text-safety layer, not a treatment. ORG-PLAN-045.

paper-mono.label: Paper Mono
paper-mono.usage: Editorial black-and-white + fine grain — the restrained default for dividers, content backgrounds, and dense sections. The diagnostic-clarity register.

navy-duotone.label: Navy Duotone
navy-duotone.usage: Signature deep-navy wash for covers + hero moments — navy shadows through slate mids to warm-paper highlights, applied transparently so the photo breathes. The structured-authority signature.

amber-graded.label: Amber Graded
amber-graded.usage: Restrained cinematic grade for section imagery — mostly neutral, a whisper of Signal Amber in the shadows and warm paper in the highlights. Kept subtle — the single amber accent per surface.

spotlight-navy.label: Spotlight Navy
spotlight-navy.usage: Dramatic monochrome + a soft radial vignette for a single hero moment — draws the eye to the subject. Use sparingly.

logo._note: Wordmark PNGs render via rsvg-convert with system-font fallback (Arial/Helvetica for Archivo). Convert wordmark SVG text to outlined paths via fontTools before final production print/PDF for exact Archivo fidelity.
